sanskrit: अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन्। प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय सम्भवाम्यात्ममायया॥
transliteration: ajo ’pi sann avyayātmā bhūtānām īśvaro ’pi san prakṛtiṃ svām adhiṣṭhāya sambhavāmy ātma-māyayā
literal_meaning: Though unborn, undecaying in essence, and the Lord of beings, I preside over my own nature and manifest by my own māyā.
contextual_meaning: The Self is never born or damaged; it chooses to appear in form to restore inner order, modeling that true identity is untouched by time or trauma.
psychological_state: Cognitive reframing from fragile ego to immutable Self, reducing existential anxiety.
trigger_scenario: A therapist reminds a client overwhelmed by aging or illness: 'The part of you that observes change never ages.'
anchor_text: Your true self is birthless and changeless; it simply chooses to appear.
